channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korean police question presidential security chief as rift over detaining president deepens
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korean police questioned the chief of the presidential security service on Friday as the two agencies clashed over attempts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Venezuelan President Maduro to be sworn in again despite serious doubts over election results
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — President Nicolás Maduro will extend his increasingly repressive rule over Venezuela until 2031 when he is sworn in on Friday, despite credible evidence that his opponent won the latest election and following protests against his plan to serve a third six-year term.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Moldovans grapple with outages as energy crisis grips pro-Russia region
item.description: Every time planned electrical outages are imposed on his village in southern Moldova, 73-year-old retiree Vasili Donici passes the time by solving crosswords and puzzles in a room he illuminates using a small gas lamp.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Mcgrath And Aurel Obreja, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Canada's Liberal Party to decide who will be the country's next prime minister on March 9
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's governing Liberal Party will announce the country's next prime minister on March 9 after a leadership vote that follows the resignation of Justin Trudeau this week, party leaders said late Thursday. Trudeau remains prime minister until a new leader is chosen.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: An albatross couple shares egg duty in this captivating low-drama reality show
item.description: WELLINGTON, New Zealand (AP) — It’s a reality show about a loving couple waiting to welcome their new arrival, watched by thousands of ardent fans.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Earth records hottest year ever in 2024 and the jump was so big it breached a key threshold
item.description: Global temperatures in 2024 soared to yet another record level, but this time it was such a big jump that Earth temporarily passed a major symbolic climate threshold.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UK foreign policy chief says Trump is right to urge higher military spending from NATO
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Europe’s security “is on a knife-edge” and President-elect Donald Trump is right to say NATO member nations must increase military spending, Britain’s top diplomat said Thursday. Foreign Secretary David Lammy said that “the post-Cold War peace is well and truly over.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama marks Martyrs' Day as Trump threatens to retake control of Panama Canal
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — A Panamanian national holiday remembering the killing of 21 protesters by police and U.S. troops in 1964 is taking on new resonance this year as U.S. President-elect Donald Trump threatens to take back control of the Panama Canal. On Jan. 9, 1964, students protested in the then-U.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump says he's working on arranging a meeting with Putin
item.description: President-elect Trump said Thursday he's working to set up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin. "He wants to meet. And we’re setting it up," Trump told Fox News's Peter Doocy at Mar-a-Lago during a meeting with Republican governors.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Harris, Emhoff cancel trip to Singapore, Middle East, Germany amid California fires
item.description: Vice President Harris and second gentleman Doug Emhoff announced their decision to cancel their upcoming international trips on Thursday in the wake of the Los Angeles wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trudeau: Trump talk of annexing Canada a distraction, won't happen
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Thursday he thinks President-elect Trump’s public musings about annexing the neighbor to the north is a distraction from Trump’s threat to impose steep tariffs on Canadian imports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Musk uses X livestream to amplify German far-right leader's views ahead of an election
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Tech billionaire Elon Musk livestreamed his chat with a leader of Germany's far-right party on Thursday, using the power of his social media platform, X, to amplify the party's message ahead of an upcoming national election — and raising concerns across Europe about the world's
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz liberation commemoration
item.description: Poland passed a resolution to ensure the safe participation of Israeli representatives who attend 80th anniversary commemorations of the liberation of Auschwitz-Birkenau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Democrats call on ICC to rescind arrest warrants for Israeli leaders
item.description: A group of House Democrats is urging the International Criminal Court (ICC) to rescind arrest warrants for Israel’s prime minister and former defense minister as Congress is poised to potentially implement sanctions in response to the global court’s actions. Reps. Eugene Vindman (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 46,000 Palestinians have been killed in the Israel-Hamas war, Gaza's Health Ministry said Thursday, with no end in sight to the 15-month conflict. The ministry said a total of 46,006 Palestinians have been killed and 109,378 wounded.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scientists drill nearly 2 miles down to pull 1.2 million-year-old ice core from Antarctic
item.description: An international team of scientists announced Thursday they’ve successfully drilled one of the oldest ice cores yet, penetrating nearly 2 miles (2.8 kilometers) to Antarctic bedrock to reach ice they say is at least 1.2 million years old.
item.dc:creator: PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House passes ICC sanctions bill; GOP Senate leader promises to bring it to floor
item.description: The House on Thursday passed legislation to sanction officials with the International Criminal Court (ICC), pushing back against the court's decision to issue arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his former defense minister for their actions during Israel’s war against H
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Outgoing ICE director: Biden should have tightened border security sooner
item.description: The outgoing head of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement Agency (ICE) said in an interview Wednesday that President Biden should have tightened border security sooner.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Polish government adopts resolution protecting Netanyahu from arrest if he attends Auschwitz event
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — The Polish government adopted a resolution on Thursday vowing to ensure the free and safe participation of the highest representatives of Israel — including Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu — who choose to attend commemorations for the 80th anniversary of the liberation of Aus
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Joe Rogan: US should take over Mexico after Canada
item.description: Podcaster Joe Rogan floated the possibility of the United States annexing Mexico after taking control of Canada, an idea president-elect Trump has suggested his administration could pursue.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: Trump expected to tap GOP House Foreign Affairs Africa director for NSC post
item.description: President-elect Trump is expected to tap Joe Foltz, staff director for the Republican side of the House Foreign Affairs Subcommittee on Africa, to lead the Africa desk at the National Security Council (NSC), according to a congressional aide.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Baby girl born on packed migrant boat heading for Spain's Canary Islands
item.description: A baby girl was born on a packed migrant boat headed for Spain's Lanzarote island in the Canary Islands. Authorities said the baby and her mother were in good condition.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italy's Meloni hopes to attend Trump inauguration as she downplays his Greenland and Panama comments
item.description: Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni says she doesn't believe President-elect Donald Trump actually intends to use military force to seize control of Greenland or the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mozambique opposition leader returns from self-imposed exile as police fire tear gas at supporters
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique's main opposition leader returned from self-imposed exile on Thursday clutching a bible and saying he still rejects the results of a disputed election last year that has sparked more than two months of protests and a violent crackdown by security forces.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Attack on Chad's presidential palace leaves 19 dead
item.description: N'DJAMENA, Chad (AP) — An attack on Chad's presidential palace left 18 assailants dead and six in custody, with one soldier killed and three wounded, state media reported Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Japan's Ishiba visiting Malaysia and Indonesia to strengthen defense and economic ties
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba is visiting Malaysia and Indonesia as part of his effort to further strengthen defense and economic ties with Southeast Asia as threats from China rise in the region.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader rallies support in Panama ahead of Maduro inauguration
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Days before Venezuela’s presidential inauguration, self-exiled Venezuelan opposition leader Edmundo González was in Panama Wednesday rallying regional support for the opposition's claims that he won the July election against President Nicolás Maduro.
item.dc:creator: JUAN ZAMORANO and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Venezuela's ultimate political survivor Nicolás Maduro faces his toughest challenge yet
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro is facing more international rebuke than at any time in his 12 years in power. The self-declared socialist is widely believed to have lost last year's election by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans jailed in Venezuela pose a test of Trump's deal-making foreign policy
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro will be sworn in for a third term Friday while hundreds of government opponents arrested since his disputed reelection last summer languish in the country's packed prisons. Sharing cells with them are as many as 10 Americans.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK lawmakers vote against inquiry into 'rape gang scandal' as Musk keeps up pressure
item.description: British lawmakers voted against an amendment to hold a national inquiry into the U.K. grooming scandal, amid international pressure from Elon Musk and others.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Americans arrested in Venezuela on eve of Maduro inauguration over ‘terrorism’ claims
item.description: Two Americans have been arrested in Venezuela, with President Nicolas Maduro claiming the charges relate to an intent to "practice terrorism against the Venezuelan people.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 23-year-old hiker found after surviving for 2 weeks in Australian mountain range
item.description: A medical student from Melbourne who was missing in a remote Australian mountain range for two weeks survived on two muesli bars, foraged berries and creek water, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
